title: macrolobium costaricense
intro: Macrolobium costaricense is a medicinal plant species native to Central America, commonly known as "Costa Rican tree" or "Panama tree", characterized by its large leaves and distinctive growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Macrolobium costaricense include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties. Herbal preparations of Macrolobium costaricense typically involve drying and decocting its leaves to create teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
